chief_complaint: A 20-year-old woman presented with cyclical passage of blood per rectum coinciding with menstruation.
history: The patient's symptoms began approximately 2 weeks after undergoing a dilatation and curettage procedure performed for second-trimester miscarriage. She had no significant past medical history and was not taking any medications.
exam: Vital signs were within normal limits. Physical examination revealed a normal abdominal examination with no signs of peritonitis or pelvic mass.
labs: MRI revealed a fistulous tract of 5 mm between the posterior uterine wall and anterior rectal wall.
